federation.note: The media-sector listening-history / scrobble RDF model — the data model a Web Scrobbler→Solid fork writes one-per-play to a pod. MINTS NOTHING: a scrobble is a media:PlaybackEvent (from @jeswr/solid-federation-vocab's media sector) referencing a media:Track credited to a media:Artist and optionally on a media:Album, with a reified played-at time:Instant (core:atTime) and the listener WebID (core:hadParticipant); it reuses media:/core:/dct:/foaf:/time: verbatim. As an ONTOLOGY TEST RIG, modelling real scrobble data surfaced media-sector gaps — per-event completion-fraction + in-progress-vs-completed status, source-service attribution (media:playedVia), loved/skipped flags, and an artist display-name term — written up as fedcon:Proposal CANDIDATES in listening.proposals.ttl / DECISIONS.md rather than edited into the sector. Ships a focused SHACL shape + an owner-only fail-closed WAC ACL helper (scrobbles are owner-private). GitHub-installable now (committed dist/, ignore-scripts=true); npm publish is a deferred migration. The media:/core: IRIs are legacy w3id.org/jeswr identifiers that re-mint to the jeswr.org persistent-ID layer per the staged migration; fedcon: is already jeswr.org-rooted.
